title: Orbalisk
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Citation}} {{Species |image=[[File:Orbalisks.jpg]] |name=Orbalisk |designation=[[Sentience/Legends|Non-sentient]] |class= |subspecies= |races= |height= |length= |wingspan= |mass= |skincolor=Varies from specimen to specimen |haircolor= |feathers= |eyecolor= |distinctions=[[Lightsaber/Legends|Lightsaber]]-immune carapace''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'' |lifespan= |origin=[[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]]
[[Kintan/Legends|Kintan]] |habitat= |diet= |language= }} {{Quote|They are called orbalisks. Creatures that feed on the power of the dark side.|[[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]]|}} An '''orbalisk''' was a [[Parasite/Legends|parasitic]] creature found on [[Dxun/Legends|Dxun]] that lived in groups. They lay inactive in caves or other dark places, until they found a suitable host creature on whose skin they would attach themselves to in order to feed. Orbalisks, once attached to a host, would multiply and grow, eventually enveloping and suffocating their victim. However, [[Freedon Nadd's holocron|the holocron]] of [[Freedon Nadd]] contained the knowledge to make armament to prevent the orbalisks from covering the face, hands, and feet of the host, allowing them to keep [[Orbalisk armor|this armor]] hidden. Perhaps by nature or as a result of residing on Dxun (which was itself a great focus of [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] power), orbalisks were able to feed on the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitivity]] of [[darksider]]s. It was said that the orbalisk armor's durability was such that [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]] had a hard time penetrating it, making a wearer basically immune to lightsaber attacks. The only weakness to this armor were the exposed joints at the neck and wrists, but these gaps were so small a lightsaber could only barely slice through. However, orbalisk armor was unable to protect the wearer from electricity, as [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] discovered when he was attacked by [[Umbaran/Legends|Umbaran]] Shadow Assassins wielding [[Force pike/Legends|Force pikes]]. However, the Orbalisks did offer some limited protection from the electrical shocks; despite being set to kill, the force pikes did not deliver a strong enough charge to subdue Bane. Orbalisks caused the wearer to feel pain, fueling the wearers dark side power and if they were removed or killed, they would release a highly potent toxin into the host's body. ==History== {{Quote|They feel the power of the dark side within you.|[[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] explains the orbalisks reaction to [[Darth Zannah/Legends|Zannah's]] touch|}} [[File:DarthBane-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|200px|Darth Bane in his shell of orbalisk armor.]] "Orbalisk" was a [[Nikto/Legends|Nikto]] word, suggesting the creatures might have originated in [[Kintan/Legends|Kintan]]. Orbalisks were poisonous to average beings, and caused extreme physical pain to their host. [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]], however, was able to remain alive by allowing the orbalisks to feed off the dark side energy in his body. In turn, the orbalisks sent a constant massive surge of adrenaline and other enzymes into his bloodstream, increasing his already considerable physical strength and enabling him to draw on even more dark side energy. However, this cycle could cause Bane to go into a fit of mindless rage if he did not keep his anger under control. The orbalisks also released other chemicals that enabled Bane to heal almost instantly from virtually any wound, even those inflicted by a lightsaber. Because of their benefits to their host, the orbalisks in a sense shared a symbiotic rather than parasitic relationship with Bane. Only electricity proved to be dangerous to the orbalisk and its host—if at a high enough voltage—although orbalisks attached to Darth Bane could harmlessly absorb over a million volts. When orbalisks died, they released toxins into the body of the host killing him/her within days or hours (depending on the strength of the individual). Orbalisks could be removed from the host, but the task was both challenging and could potentially kill the host. The host had to be in very good health in order to even attempt it. The first crucial step to remove a living orbalisk was to send an electric jolt—powerful enough to stun the orbalisk but not harm its host—through its small underbelly, resulting in the orbalisk weakening the adhesive it used to bind itself onto the host. After the orbalisks were removed, they could be disposed of by electrocution. For a period of 10 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|years]], Bane wore orbalisks that had attached themselves to him as living armor. In [[990 BBY]], [[Caleb]] [[Confrontation on Ambria|removed]] many of the orbalisks covering Bane's body with such a method after many of the parasites were killed by the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord's]] own [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] in a [[Duel on Tython|duel]] against five [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]]. When the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] was [[Declaration of a New Republic/Legends|founded]] [[4 ABY/Legends|nearly a millennium later]], the [[Coruscant Livestock Exchange and Exhibition]] featured these creatures along with [[energy spider]]s and [[Droch/Legends|drochs]] in its arthropod exhibit.''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]'' ==Behind the scenes== Orbalisks first appeared in [[Kevin J. Anderson]]'s short story, ''[[Bane of the Sith]]'' in 2001. ''Bane of the Sith'' was adapted into part of [[Drew Karpyshyn]]'s novel, ''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]''. The Orbalisks play a major part in the story of the novel and its sequel, ''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]''. They are also seen in [[Legacy (2006) 5|''Legacy'' (2006) 5]], when [[Darth Krayt]] consults the [[Holocron/Legends|holocrons]] of [[Holocron of Heresies|Darth Andeddu]], [[Darth Nihilus' holocron|Darth Nihilus]] and [[Darth Bane's holocron|Darth Bane]], as the [[Gatekeeper/Legends|gatekeeper]] of Bane's holocron appears wearing orbalisk armor. ==Appearances== *[[Cartel Market|''Star Wars: The Old Republic'' Cartel Market]] *''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Rule of Two (audiobook)}} *{{GamerCite|3|Bane of the Sith|reprint=1}} {{1st}} *''[[Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil]]'' {{Ab|Darth Bane: Dynasty of Evil (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] {{Ab|Darth Plagueis (audiobook)}} {{Mo}} *[[Legacy (2006) 5|''Legacy'' (2006) 5]] {{Holocron}} ==Sources== *''[[Star Wars: Secrets of the Sith]]'' {{1stm}} {{1stp}} *''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' *''[[The Wildlife of Star Wars: A Field Guide]]'' *''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]'' *''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' *''[[Threats of the Galaxy]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' *''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' *[[Star Wars: Force and Destiny Beginner Game|''Star Wars: Force and Destiny'' Beginner Game]] {{Po}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |bg=Орбалиск |de=Orbalisk |es=Orbalisko |pl=Orbalisk }} [[Category:Arthropods]] [[Category:Blaster-resistant creatures]] [[Category:Creatures of Dxun]] [[Category:Jungle and forest creatures]] [[Category:Lightsaber-resistant creatures]] [[Category:Non-sentient species]] [[Category:Parasitic creatures]]
